questionParts: Compute the partial derivative with respect to x, {{f_x = \frac{\partial f}{\partial x}}}
explanationParts: To find {{f_x}}, treat {{y}} as a constant and differentiate with respect to {{x}}.
The derivative of {{4x^3y}} with respect to {{x}} is {{(3 \cdot 4x^{3-1})y = 12x^2y}}.
The derivative of {{-7x}} with respect to {{x}} is {{-7}}.
The derivative of {{2y^6}} with respect to {{x}} is {{0}} (since {{y}} is treated as a constant).

questionParts: Compute the partial derivative with respect to y, {{f_y = \frac{\partial f}{\partial y}}}
explanationParts: To find {{f_y}}, treat {{x}} as a constant and differentiate with respect to {{y}}.
The derivative of {{4x^3y}} with respect to {{y}} is {{4x^3(1) = 4x^3}}.
The derivative of {{-7x}} with respect to {{y}} is {{0}} (since {{x}} is treated as a constant).
The derivative of {{2y^6}} with respect to {{y}} is {{(6 \cdot 2y^{6-1}) = 12y^5}}.

questionParts: Compute the partial derivative with respect to x, {{g_x = \frac{\partial g}{\partial x}}}
explanationParts: To find {{g_x}}, treat {{y}} as a constant and differentiate with respect to {{x}}.
The derivative of {{3x^2y^3}} with respect to {{x}} is {{(2 \cdot 3x^{2-1})y^3 = 6xy^3}}.
The derivative of {{\cos(y)}} with respect to {{x}} is {{0}} (since {{y}} is treated as a constant).

questionParts: Compute the partial derivative with respect to y, {{h_y = \frac{\partial h}{\partial y}}}
explanationParts: To find {{h_y}}, treat {{x}} as a constant and differentiate with respect to {{y}}.
The derivative of {{5\sin(x)}} with respect to {{y}} is {{0}} (since {{x}} is treated as a constant).
The derivative of {{x^4y^2}} with respect to {{y}} is {{x^4(2y^{2-1}) = 2x^4y}}.
